questions.question: Which article of the Constitution defines India as a Union of States?
questions.explanation: Article 1 states that 'India, that is Bharat, shall be a Union of States.'

questions.question: Which article of the Constitution abolishes untouchability?
questions.explanation: Article 17 abolishes untouchability in any form and makes its practice a punishable offence.

questions.question: Which article of the Constitution provides for the right against self-incrimination?
questions.explanation: Article 20(3) provides that no person accused of an offence shall be compelled to be a witness against himself.

questions.question: Untouchability is abolished and its practice is forbidden under:
questions.explanation: Article 17: 'Untouchability is abolished and its practice in any form is forbidden. The enforcement of any disability arising out of untouchability shall be an offence punishable by law.' The Protection of Civil Rights Act 1955 penalises untouchability.

questions.question: The Constitution describes India as:
questions.explanation: Article 1 describes India as a 'Union of States' — not federation. This implies the union is indestructible and states cannot secede. India is not a true federation.

questions.question: Article 17 abolishes:
questions.explanation: Article 17 abolishes untouchability and its practice in any form is an offense punishable by law. The Protection of Civil Rights Act 1955 and SC/ST (Prevention of Atrocities) Act 1989 implement this.

questions.question: Articles 23 and 24 deal with:
questions.explanation: Articles 23-24 (Right against Exploitation): Art. 23 prohibits human trafficking and forced labor (begar). Art. 24 prohibits employment of children below 14 years in factories/mines/hazardous work.

questions.question: Article 15 prohibits discrimination on grounds of:
options: Religion, race, caste, sex, or place of birth
questions.explanation: Article 15 prohibits discrimination by the State on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex, OR place of birth. However, special provisions for women, children, SCs, STs, OBCs are allowed.

questions.question: The National Commission for Backward Classes is established under:
questions.explanation: 102nd Constitutional Amendment (2018) inserted Article 338B, giving constitutional status to the National Commission for Backward Classes (NCBC). Previously it was a statutory body under 1993 Act.

questions.question: The Central Vigilance Commission (CVC) was established by:
options: Executive order in 1964 and later CVC Act 2003
questions.explanation: CVC was established by executive order in 1964 (on recommendation of Santhanam Committee on Corruption Prevention). It was given statutory status by the CVC Act 2003.

questions.question: Jammu & Kashmir was reorganized into two Union Territories in:
questions.explanation: J&K Reorganization Act 2019 bifurcated the state of J&K into: (1) J&K UT (with legislature), (2) Ladakh UT (without legislature), effective 31 October 2019. Article 370 was also abrogated.

questions.question: Article 371 deals with:
options: Special provisions for certain states (like NE states)
questions.explanation: Articles 371-371J provide special provisions for certain states: 371A (Nagaland), 371B (Assam), 371C (Manipur), 371D-F (Andhra Pradesh/Telangana), 371G (Mizoram), 371H (Arunachal), 371I (Goa), 371J (Karnataka).

questions.question: Which Article prohibits traffic in human beings and forced labour?
questions.explanation: Article 23 prohibits trafficking in human beings, begar (forced labour) and other similar forms of forced labour. Any contravention is an offence punishable by law.

questions.question: Which Article prohibits employment of children below 14 years in hazardous work?
questions.explanation: Article 24 prohibits employment of children below 14 years in factories, mines or other hazardous employment. The Child Labour (Prohibition & Regulation) Act implements this.

questions.question: Protection against self-incrimination is guaranteed by:
questions.explanation: Article 20(3) states no person accused of any offence shall be compelled to be a witness against himself. This is the right against self-incrimination (nemo tenetur seipsum accusare).

questions.question: Under which Articles are protection against arbitrary arrest and detention provided?
questions.explanation: Article 22 provides protection against arbitrary arrest: right to be informed of grounds, right to consult a lawyer, production before magistrate within 24 hours, and preventive detention limits.

questions.question: The Central Vigilance Commission (CVC) was given statutory status by:
questions.explanation: The Central Vigilance Commission was established in 1964 by executive resolution based on Santhanam Committee recommendations. It was given statutory status by the CVC Act, 2003.

questions.question: The Lokpal Act was passed in:
questions.explanation: The Lokpal and Lokayuktas Act was passed in December 2013. India's first Lokpal (Justice Pinaki Chandra Ghose) was appointed in March 2019 — nearly 6 years after the Act was passed.

questions.question: Article 1 of the Indian Constitution describes India as:
questions.explanation: Article 1 describes India as a 'Union of States' — not a federation. This term was deliberately chosen to indicate that States have no right to secede from the Union (unlike a federation or confederation).

questions.question: Article 17 of the Constitution abolishes:
questions.explanation: Article 17 abolishes untouchability and forbids its practice in any form. Enforcement of any disability arising out of untouchability shall be an offence. The Protection of Civil Rights Act, 1955 implements this.

questions.question: The 6th Schedule of the Constitution provides for:
options: Administration of Tribal Areas in Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura, and Mizoram
Scheduled Areas and Scheduled Tribes in general
questions.explanation: The 6th Schedule provides for autonomous District Councils and Regional Councils for tribal areas in Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura, and Mizoram — giving tribal communities substantial self-governance powers.

questions.question: The maximum number of members in a State Legislative Council (Vidhan Parishad) cannot exceed:
options: One-third of the total members of the State Legislative Assembly
One-fourth of the total members of the State Assembly
questions.explanation: Article 171 states that the total number of members in a Vidhan Parishad shall not exceed one-third of the total members of the Vidhan Sabha, and shall not be less than 40 members.

questions.question: Which constitutional amendment introduced the Goods and Services Tax (GST)?
questions.explanation: The 101st Constitutional Amendment Act (2016) introduced GST in India by amending Article 246A and others.

questions.question: Which Finance Commission is constituted to recommend devolution of taxes between Centre and States under Article 280?
questions.explanation: Article 280 provides for the constitution of a Finance Commission every 5 years (or earlier if needed) to recommend the distribution of net proceeds of taxes between the Centre and States.

questions.question: The total number of Articles in the original Indian Constitution was:
questions.explanation: The original Constitution had 395 Articles, 8 Schedules, and 22 Parts. Currently it has around 448 Articles and 12 Schedules due to amendments.

questions.question: UPSC (Union Public Service Commission) conducts examinations for:
options: All India Services and Central Services Group A & B (civil services exam, IAS, IPS, IFS, etc.)
questions.explanation: UPSC (Article 315) conducts Civil Services Examination (IAS, IPS, IFS), combined defence services, engineering services, NDA, etc. for Group A and B Central Services and All India Services. State PSCs conduct state service exams.

questions.question: Under Article 352, National Emergency can be proclaimed on grounds of:
options: War, External Aggression, or Armed Rebellion
questions.explanation: National Emergency (Article 352) can be proclaimed on grounds of: (i) War, (ii) External Aggression, or (iii) Armed Rebellion (formerly 'internal disturbance', changed by 44th Amendment).

questions.question: The 44th Constitutional Amendment (1978) changed the term 'Internal Disturbance' in Article 352 to:
questions.explanation: 44th Amendment (1978) changed 'internal disturbance' to 'armed rebellion' in Article 352, making it harder to impose National Emergency on minor internal disturbances.

questions.question: The National Commission for Scheduled Castes is a:
questions.explanation: Article 338 establishes the National Commission for Scheduled Castes as a constitutional body. It investigates matters relating to constitutional and statutory safeguards for SCs.

questions.question: The concept of 'Inter-State Council' is provided in:
questions.explanation: Article 263 provides for an Inter-State Council for coordinating relations between states and between states and the Centre. The Inter-State Council was set up in 1990.

questions.question: How many types of majority are needed for Constitutional Amendments?
questions.explanation: Constitutional amendments require: (1) Simple majority (for some provisions like Article 5), (2) Special majority: 2/3rd present and voting + majority of total membership (most amendments), (3) Special majority + ratification by half the states.

questions.question: Part II of the Constitution (Articles 5-11) deals with:
questions.explanation: Part II (Articles 5-11) deals with Citizenship — at the commencement of the Constitution. The details of citizenship are governed by the Citizenship Act 1955.

questions.question: Article 16(4) allows:
options: Reservation in government jobs for backward classes
questions.explanation: Article 16(4): Nothing in Article 16 shall prevent the State from making provision for reservation of appointments for any backward class of citizens not adequately represented in services.

questions.question: The EWS (Economically Weaker Sections) 10% reservation was introduced by:
questions.explanation: 103rd Constitutional Amendment (January 2019) introduced 10% reservation for Economically Weaker Sections (EWS) among general category — through new Articles 15(6) and 16(6).

questions.question: GST Council is chaired by:
questions.explanation: GST Council (Article 279A, inserted by 101st Amendment 2016) is chaired by the Union Finance Minister. Members include Finance Ministers of all states.

questions.question: Reservation of seats for women in Panchayats is mandated by:
questions.explanation: Article 243D mandates reservation of not less than one-third of seats for women in Panchayats at all levels. Many States (including Tamil Nadu) have increased this to 50% by State law.

questions.question: Article 15(4) allows the State to make special provisions for:
options: Socially and educationally backward classes and SC/STs
questions.explanation: Article 15(4) — inserted by 1st Amendment (1951) — allows the State to make special provisions for the advancement of socially and educationally backward classes of citizens, SCs and STs.

questions.question: Which Article deals with the official language of the Union?
questions.explanation: Article 343 states Hindi in Devanagari script shall be the official language of the Union. English was also continued as an official language by the Official Languages Act, 1963.

questions.question: The Goods and Services Tax (GST) was introduced by the:
questions.explanation: The 101st Constitutional Amendment (2016) introduced GST — inserting Art 246A (concurrent power to levy GST), Art 269A (IGST), Art 279A (GST Council), and amending Schedule 6/7.

questions.question: The two-party system in Tamil Nadu politics refers to:
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu has a unique two-Dravidian party system where DMK and AIADMK have alternated in power since 1967. National parties like INC and BJP have been junior alliance partners rather than independent power centers.

questions.question: Which article of the Constitution protects persons from double jeopardy?
questions.explanation: Article 20(2) provides that no person shall be prosecuted and punished for the same offence more than once — protection against double jeopardy.

questions.question: Which article of the Constitution prohibits child labour (below 14 years) in factories and hazardous employment?
questions.explanation: Article 24 prohibits employment of children below 14 years in factories, mines, or other hazardous employment.

questions.question: The official language of the Union of India is:
questions.explanation: Article 343: Hindi in Devanagari script is the official language of the Union. English continues to be used for official purposes under Article 343(2). India has no 'national language'.

questions.question: The official language Hindi was to replace English by:
questions.explanation: Article 343(2) provided that English would continue for 15 years until 1965. But due to protests (especially in Tamil Nadu), the Official Languages Act 1963 made English continue indefinitely.

questions.question: The Right to Information Act was passed in:
questions.explanation: The Right to Information (RTI) Act 2005 was passed to provide citizens access to information held by public authorities, making governance more transparent and accountable.

questions.question: The Lokpal and Lokayuktas Act was passed in:
questions.explanation: The Lokpal and Lokayuktas Act 2013 established the Lokpal at the Union level and Lokayuktas at the state level for investigation of corruption charges against public servants.

questions.question: Which of the following is a feature of the Preamble to the Indian Constitution?
options: It is enforceable in a court of law
It declares India as a Socialist, Secular, Democratic Republic
It lists the Fundamental Duties of citizens
questions.explanation: The Preamble declares India as a Sovereign, Socialist, Secular, Democratic Republic. It is not enforceable in court.

questions.question: Under the Indian Constitution, which article guarantees the Right to Equality?
questions.explanation: Article 14 guarantees equality before the law and equal protection of the laws.

questions.question: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act is associated with which level of governance?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment (1992) gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj Institutions (rural local self-government).

questions.question: Which of the following is a Directive Principle of State Policy under the Indian Constitution?
options: Equal pay for equal work for both men and women
questions.explanation: Equal pay for equal work is a Directive Principle under Article 39(d), guiding state policy.

questions.question: Who appoints the Chief Election Commissioner of India?
questions.explanation: The President of India appoints the Chief Election Commissioner and other Election Commissioners.

questions.question: In the Indian judiciary, the power of judicial review is vested in which court?
options: Both Supreme Court and High Courts
questions.explanation: Both the Supreme Court and High Courts have the power of judicial review to examine the constitutionality of laws.

questions.question: Which Tamil Nadu government scheme provides financial assistance to women heads of families?
questions.explanation: The Kalaignar Magalir Urimai Thittam provides monthly financial assistance to women heads of families in Tamil Nadu.

questions.question: The concept of 'Fundamental Duties' in the Indian Constitution was borrowed from which country?
questions.explanation: The Fundamental Duties (Article 51A) were inspired by the Constitution of the former USSR.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a function of a Gram Panchayat?
options: Conducting elections for the State Legislature
questions.explanation: Conducting State Legislature elections is done by the Election Commission, not by Gram Panchayats.

questions.question: The 'Right to Information Act' in India is primarily aimed at promoting which aspect of governance?
questions.explanation: The RTI Act empowers citizens to seek information from public authorities, promoting transparency and accountability.

questions.question: The concept of 'Welfare State' in the Indian Constitution is primarily reflected in which part?
questions.explanation: Directive Principles of State Policy (Part IV) enjoin the state to promote the welfare of the people by securing a social order.

questions.question: Which Part of the Indian Constitution deals with Panchayati Raj institutions?
questions.explanation: Part IX (Articles 243-243O) of the Constitution deals with Panchayats, added by the 73rd Amendment.

questions.question: The idea of 'Directive Principles of State Policy' in the Indian Constitution is borrowed from the Constitution of which country?
questions.explanation: The Directive Principles of State Policy were inspired by the Irish Constitution, which had similar provisions.

questions.question: The Right to Life and Personal Liberty is guaranteed under which Article of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 21 of the Indian Constitution guarantees protection of life and personal liberty to all persons.

questions.question: Which constitutional amendments introduced the 11th and 12th Schedules to the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment added the 11th Schedule (Panchayats), and the 74th Amendment added the 12th Schedule (Municipalities).

questions.question: The word 'Secular' was inserted into the Preamble of the Indian Constitution by which amendment?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment Act, 1976, inserted the words 'Socialist' and 'Secular' into the Preamble.

questions.question: The Governor of a state in India is appointed by whom?
questions.explanation: As per Article 155, the Governor of a state is appointed by the President of India.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution provides for a High Court in each state?
questions.explanation: Article 214 states that there shall be a High Court for each state in India.

questions.question: The Election Commission of India is a:
questions.explanation: The Election Commission is a constitutional body established under Article 324 of the Constitution.

questions.question: The Tamil Nadu government's 'Varumun Kappom Thittam' is primarily a scheme for:
questions.explanation: Varumun Kappom Thittam (Prevention of Poverty Scheme) aims to identify and assist families below the poverty line.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution provides for the establishment of a Panchayat at the village level?
questions.explanation: Article 243B specifically provides for the constitution of Panchayats at the village, intermediate and district levels.

questions.question: The Preamble of the Indian Constitution declares India to be a:
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment added 'Socialist' and 'Secular' to the Preamble, making it 'Sovereign Socialist Secular Democratic Republic'.

questions.question: Which Fundamental Right prohibits trafficking in human beings and forced labour?
questions.explanation: Article 23 under the Right against Exploitation prohibits trafficking and forced labour.

questions.question: The Directive Principles of State Policy are:
questions.explanation: Article 37 states that Directive Principles are not enforceable by any court but are fundamental in the governance of the country.

questions.question: The election to the office of the President of India is conducted by which body?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission of India is responsible for conducting elections to the offices of President and Vice-President.

questions.question: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act added which Part to the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment added Part IX (Articles 243 to 243O) dealing with Panchayats.

questions.question: The Supreme Court of India derives its powers from which Articles of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Articles 124 to 147 cover the establishment, jurisdiction and powers of the Supreme Court.

questions.question: The Chief Minister of a state is appointed by:
options: The Chief Justice of the state High Court
questions.explanation: Under Article 164, the Governor appoints the Chief Minister, who is the leader of the majority party in the state assembly.

questions.question: The concept of 'Welfare State' in the Indian Constitution is primarily reflected in:
questions.explanation: Directive Principles aim to secure social and economic justice, embodying the welfare state ideal.

questions.question: Which Tamil Nadu government scheme provides free breakfast to primary school children in government schools?
questions.explanation: The Chief Minister's Breakfast Scheme was launched in 2022 to provide free breakfast to students in government primary schools.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a feature of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: India follows parliamentary supremacy under a written constitution, not absolute parliamentary sovereignty as in the UK.

questions.question: The word 'Socialist' in the Preamble of the Indian Constitution was added by which amendment?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) added 'Socialist', 'Secular', and 'Integrity' to the Preamble.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Indian Constitution are Fundamental Rights guaranteed?
questions.explanation: Part III of the Constitution, covering Articles 12 to 35, deals with Fundamental Rights.

questions.question: Which Directive Principle is based on the Gandhian principle of promoting cottage industries?
questions.explanation: Article 43 directs the State to promote cottage industries on an individual or cooperative basis in rural areas.

questions.question: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992, added which part to the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Part IX (Articles 243 to 243-O) was inserted by the 73rd Amendment to establish Panchayati Raj institutions.

questions.question: Who conducts elections to the office of the President of India?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission of India conducts the election for the President under Article 324.

questions.question: The power of judicial review in India is vested in which court?
options: Both Supreme Court and High Courts
questions.explanation: Both the Supreme Court (Article 32) and High Courts (Article 226) have the power of judicial review.

questions.question: The minimum age for contesting elections to a Panchayat in Tamil Nadu is:
questions.explanation: As per the Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj Act, the minimum age for contesting panchayat elections is 21 years.

questions.question: Which of the following articles of the Indian Constitution deals with the establishment of Panchayats?
questions.explanation: Article 243 of the Indian Constitution provides for the definition and establishment of Panchayats under Part IX.

questions.question: Which constitutional amendment added the words 'Socialist' and 'Secular' to the Preamble of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) inserted 'Socialist', 'Secular', and 'Integrity' into the Preamble.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution prohibits discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex, or place of birth?
questions.explanation: Article 15 prohibits discrimination on these grounds, while Article 14 is equality before law.

questions.question: Which directive principle under Article 43 promotes the establishment of cottage industries in rural areas?
questions.explanation: Article 43 (Directive Principles) directs the state to promote cottage industries, a Gandhian principle.

questions.question: Which constitutional amendment granted constitutional status to Panchayati Raj institutions?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment (1992) added Part IX to the Constitution, giving constitutional status to Panchayats.

questions.question: Under which article of the Constitution is the Election Commission of India established?
questions.explanation: Article 324 vests the superintendence, direction and control of elections in the Election Commission.

questions.question: Which landmark Supreme Court case established the 'basic structure' doctrine of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: In Kesavananda Bharati (1973), the Supreme Court held that the basic structure of the Constitution cannot be amended.

questions.question: The Tamil Nadu Legislative Council was abolished in which year?
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu abolished its Legislative Council in 1986 through the Tamil Nadu Legislative Council (Abolition) Act.

questions.question: Reservation for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes in the Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly is provided under which article of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 332 provides for reservation of seats for SC/STs in the Legislative Assemblies of States.

questions.question: Which of the following is a fundamental duty under Article 51A of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 51A(e) states it is the duty of every citizen to safeguard public property and abjure violence.

questions.question: The Uniform Civil Code (UCC) is listed under which article of the Directive Principles of State Policy?
questions.explanation: Article 44 of the Constitution directs the State to secure a Uniform Civil Code for all citizens.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a feature of the Preamble to the Constitution of India?
questions.explanation: The Preamble declares India as Sovereign, Socialist, Secular, Democratic, and Republic; 'Federal' is not explicitly mentioned.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Constitution are the Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSPs) enshrined?
questions.explanation: DPSPs are contained in Part IV of the Constitution, covering Articles 36 to 51.

questions.question: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992, added which Part to the Constitution of India?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment added Part IX (Panchayats) to the Constitution, while Part IX-A deals with Municipalities.

questions.question: In the context of Panchayati Raj, what is the minimum age prescribed for a person to contest elections to a Gram Panchayat in Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: As per the Tamil Nadu Panchayats Act, 1994, the minimum age to contest Gram Panchayat elections is 21 years.

questions.question: Which of the following Fundamental Rights is available only to citizens of India and not to foreign nationals?
questions.explanation: Article 19 (freedom of speech, assembly, etc.) is specifically guaranteed only to citizens, not to foreigners.

questions.question: The concept of 'Judicial Review' in India is borrowed from the Constitution of which country?
questions.explanation: Judicial Review, the power of courts to examine the constitutionality of laws, is inspired by the US Constitution.

questions.question: Which Tamil Nadu government scheme provides financial assistance to women heads of households for education of their children?
options: Moovalur Ramamirtham Ammaiyar Higher Education Assurance Scheme
questions.explanation: The Moovalur Ramamirtham Ammaiyar Higher Education Assurance Scheme provides financial aid to girl children of women heads of households.

questions.question: The Election Commission of India is a constitutional body established under which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 324 of the Constitution provides for the Election Commission to supervise elections to Parliament and State Legislatures.

questions.question: Which of the following is a 'Writ' issued by a High Court or the Supreme Court to compel a public authority to perform a public duty?
questions.explanation: Mandamus is a writ commanding a public official or body to perform a duty that they are legally obligated to perform.

questions.question: The 'Tamil Nadu State Election Commission' is responsible for conducting elections to which of the following bodies?
questions.explanation: The State Election Commission conducts elections to local bodies (Panchayats and Municipalities) in Tamil Nadu.

questions.question: Which part of the Indian Constitution deals with Directive Principles of State Policy?
questions.explanation: Directive Principles of State Policy are contained in Part IV (Articles 36-51) of the Constitution.

questions.question: The concept of 'Basic Structure' of the Constitution was propounded in which landmark case?
questions.explanation: In Kesavananda Bharati (1973), the Supreme Court held that the basic structure of the Constitution cannot be amended.

questions.question: Which article of the Constitution provides for the establishment of Panchayats?
questions.explanation: Article 243 defines Panchayats; Part IX was added by the 73rd Amendment Act, 1992.

questions.question: The Governor of a state is appointed by whom?
questions.explanation: Article 155 states that the Governor is appointed by the President of India.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a Fundamental Right?
questions.explanation: Right to Property (Article 31) was removed from Fundamental Rights by the 44th Amendment and is now a legal right under Article 300A.

questions.question: The Election Commission of India is a constitutional body under which article?
questions.explanation: Article 324 vests the superintendence, direction, and control of elections in the Election Commission.

questions.question: Which Tamil Nadu government scheme provides financial assistance to women for childbirth?
options: Dr. Muthulakshmi Reddy Maternity Benefit Scheme
questions.explanation: The Dr. Muthulakshmi Reddy Maternity Benefit Scheme provides financial assistance to pregnant women in Tamil Nadu.

questions.question: Which writ is issued to prevent a lower court from exceeding its jurisdiction?
questions.explanation: Writ of Prohibition is issued by a higher court to prevent a lower court from acting beyond its jurisdiction.

questions.question: The 73rd Amendment Act, 1992 added which part to the Constitution?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment added Part IX (Panchayats) and the 74th Amendment added Part IXA (Municipalities).

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution provides for the establishment of Panchayati Raj institutions?
questions.explanation: Part IX (Article 243) was added by the 73rd Amendment to create Panchayati Raj institutions.

questions.question: The concept of 'Welfare State' in the Indian Constitution is reflected in which part?
questions.explanation: Directive Principles (Part IV) embody the welfare state philosophy by directing the state to promote social and economic welfare.

questions.question: The 73rd Amendment Act, 1992 added which new part to the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment added Part IX (The Panchayats); the 74th Amendment added Part IX-A for Municipalities.

questions.question: Which of the following rights is not available to foreign nationals in India?
options: Right to life and personal liberty
questions.explanation: Article 19 rights (freedom of speech, etc.) are guaranteed only to citizens, not foreign nationals.

questions.question: The 'Basic Structure' doctrine of the Indian Constitution was propounded in which landmark case?
questions.explanation: In Kesavananda Bharati (1973), the Supreme Court held that Parliament cannot alter the basic structure of the Constitution.

questions.question: Which schedule of the Indian Constitution contains provisions regarding the administration of tribal areas?
questions.explanation: The Sixth Schedule provides for autonomous councils in tribal areas of northeastern states.

questions.question: The maximum interval between two sessions of Parliament as per Article 85 is?
questions.explanation: Article 85(1) mandates that not more than six months shall elapse between the last sitting of one session and the first sitting of the next.

questions.question: Which Tamil Nadu local body election reservation policy is mandated by the 73rd Amendment?
options: Reservation for SC/ST in proportion to population
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment mandates reservation of seats for SC/ST in proportion to population and at least one-third (33%) for women.

questions.question: The Election Commission of India is constituted under which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 324 establishes the Election Commission to superintend elections to Parliament, state legislatures, and offices of President and Vice-President.

questions.question: Who appoints the Chief Minister of a state in India?
questions.explanation: As per Article 164, the Governor appoints the Chief Minister as the constitutional head of the state.

questions.question: Which of the following is a feature of the Indian Constitution borrowed from the Government of India Act, 1935?
questions.explanation: The federal scheme, including the office of Governor and the federal judiciary, was largely borrowed from the Government of India Act, 1935.

questions.question: The word 'Socialist' in the Preamble of the Indian Constitution implies which of the following?
options: Nationalization of all means of production
questions.explanation: 'Socialist' in the Indian context means a mixed economy where the state aims to reduce inequality and ensure social and economic justice, not full nationalization.

questions.question: Which of the following Fundamental Rights is available only to Indian citizens and not to foreign nationals?
options: Right to protection of life and personal liberty
Right to freedom of speech and expression
questions.explanation: Article 19 (freedom of speech, assembly, etc.) is guaranteed only to citizens, while rights like Articles 14 and 21 are available to all persons.

questions.question: Under the Directive Principles of State Policy, which article directs the State to secure a uniform civil code for all citizens?
questions.explanation: Article 44 of the Indian Constitution directs the State to endeavor to secure a Uniform Civil Code throughout the territory of India.

questions.question: In the Panchayati Raj system, the 'Gram Sabha' consists of which of the following?
options: All adult persons registered as voters in the village
Members of the Zilla Parishad from that area
questions.explanation: The Gram Sabha is a body consisting of all persons registered as voters in the electoral roll of the village panchayat area.

questions.question: The Constitution (73rd Amendment) Act, 1992 added which new part to the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment added Part IX (The Panchayats) to the Constitution, while Part IX-A was added by the 74th Amendment for Municipalities.

questions.question: Which of the following is a function of the Election Commission of India?
options: Appointment of the Chief Election Commissioner
Conduct of elections to Parliament and State Legislatures
questions.explanation: The Election Commission is responsible for the superintendence, direction, and conduct of elections to Parliament, State Legislatures, and the offices of President and Vice-President.

questions.question: The power of judicial review in India is exercised by which of the following?
options: Both the Supreme Court and High Courts
The Supreme Court, High Courts, and District Courts
questions.explanation: Judicial review is the power of both the Supreme Court (under Article 32) and High Courts (under Article 226) to examine the constitutionality of laws.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key feature of the Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj Act?
options: Direct election of the District Collector
Reservation of seats for women up to 25%
Three-tier system with District Panchayat as the apex
Abolition of the Village Panchayat level
questions.explanation: The Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj Act provides for a three-tier system: Village Panchayat, Panchayat Union, and District Panchayat.

questions.question: Which of the following amendments to the Indian Constitution is known as the 'Mini-Constitution'?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) made extensive changes to the Constitution, including adding the words 'Socialist', 'Secular', and 'Integrity' to the Preamble.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Indian Constitution can the President of India promulgate an ordinance when Parliament is not in session?
questions.explanation: Article 123 empowers the President to promulgate ordinances during recess of Parliament.

questions.question: Which of the following is a Directive Principle of State Policy?
questions.explanation: Uniform Civil Code is a Directive Principle under Article 44, while others are Fundamental Rights.

questions.question: The 73rd Amendment Act of 1992 is related to:
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj institutions.

questions.question: The concept of 'Judicial Review' in India is borrowed from which country's constitution?
questions.explanation: Judicial review is inspired by the US Constitution, though adapted to Indian conditions.

questions.question: Which of the following is a special feature of the Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj system?
options: Direct election of Gram Panchayat Presidents
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu follows a three-tier system with reservations for women and direct election of village panchayat presidents.

questions.question: The 'Right to Information Act' was enacted in India in the year:
questions.explanation: The RTI Act was passed in 2005 to promote transparency.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution deals with the abolition of untouchability?
questions.explanation: Article 17 abolishes untouchability and forbids its practice in any form.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a feature of the Indian Constitution's Preamble?
questions.explanation: The Preamble declares India as Sovereign, Socialist, Secular, Democratic, Republic, but does not use the term 'Federal'.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Constitution is the Right to Constitutional Remedies guaranteed?
questions.explanation: Article 32 provides the right to move the Supreme Court for enforcement of Fundamental Rights.

questions.question: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act relates to which of the following?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment (1992) introduced Part IX and the Eleventh Schedule for Panchayati Raj.

questions.question: The Election Commission of India is established under which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 324 vests the superintendence, direction, and control of elections in the Election Commission.

questions.question: Which of the following is a landmark judgment related to the Basic Structure Doctrine?
questions.explanation: In Kesavananda Bharati (1973), the Supreme Court propounded the basic structure doctrine.

questions.question: The Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj Act was enacted in which year?
questions.explanation: The Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj Act was passed in 1994 to conform to the 73rd Amendment.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a writ issued by the Supreme Court or High Courts?
questions.explanation: Summons is not a constitutional writ; the five writs are Habeas Corpus, Mandamus, Prohibition, Certiorari, and Quo Warranto.

questions.question: The concept of 'Gram Sabha' is enshrined in which part of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Part IX (Article 243A) provides for Gram Sabha as the basic unit of Panchayati Raj.

questions.question: Which Tamil Nadu government scheme provides free bus travel for women?
options: Free Bus Travel Scheme for Women
questions.explanation: The Tamil Nadu government launched a free bus travel scheme for women in 2021.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a feature of the Preamble to the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Preamble declares India as Sovereign, Socialist, Secular, and Democratic Republic; 'Federal' is not explicitly mentioned.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Indian Constitution are the Directive Principles of State Policy enumerated?
questions.explanation: Directive Principles are contained in Part IV, Articles 36 to 51 of the Constitution.

questions.question: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992, added which new Part to the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Part IX was added for Panchayats; Part IX-A was added later for Municipalities by the 74th Amendment.

questions.question: In the context of Tamil Nadu, which of the following is a key feature of the 'Varumun Kappom Thittam' (Preventive Health Scheme)?
questions.explanation: Varumun Kappom Thittam is a Tamil Nadu government scheme for universal health screening and early detection of diseases.

questions.question: The power of judicial review in India is derived from which of the following?
questions.explanation: Judicial review is implied from Article 13 (laws inconsistent with fundamental rights), Article 32 (Supreme Court), and Article 226 (High Courts).

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a function of the Election Commission of India?
options: Appointment of the Chief Election Commissioner
questions.explanation: The Chief Election Commissioner is appointed by the President of India, not by the Election Commission.

questions.question: The concept of 'Basic Structure' of the Constitution was propounded by the Supreme Court in which landmark case?
questions.explanation: The Basic Structure doctrine was established in the Kesavananda Bharati case (1973), limiting Parliament's amending power.

questions.question: Under the Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj system, the District Planning Committee is constituted under which Article of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 243-ZD mandates the constitution of District Planning Committees to consolidate plans prepared by Panchayats and Municipalities.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the establishment of Panchayats?
questions.explanation: Article 243 defines Panchayats; Part IX was added by the 73rd Amendment.

questions.question: The concept of 'Directive Principles of State Policy' is borrowed from which country's constitution?
questions.explanation: DPSP were inspired by the Irish Constitution, which itself borrowed from the Spanish Constitution.

questions.question: Which schedule of the Indian Constitution contains provisions regarding the administration of scheduled areas and tribes?
questions.explanation: The Fifth Schedule deals with administration of Scheduled Areas and Tribes except in northeastern states (covered by Sixth Schedule).

questions.question: Which Fundamental Right is described as the 'heart and soul' of the Indian Constitution by Dr. B.R. Ambedkar?
questions.explanation: Ambedkar called Article 32 (Right to Constitutional Remedies) the heart and soul of the Constitution.

questions.question: The 'Doctrine of Basic Structure' was propounded by the Supreme Court in which landmark case?
questions.explanation: In 1973, the Supreme Court in Kesavananda Bharati held that Parliament cannot amend the basic structure of the Constitution.

questions.question: Under the 73rd Amendment, the minimum age for contesting Panchayat elections is?
questions.explanation: Article 243F sets the minimum age at 21 years for Panchayat elections, unlike 25 for Parliament/State Legislature.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a feature mentioned in the Preamble of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Preamble declares India as Sovereign, Socialist, Secular, Democratic, Republic; 'Federal' is not mentioned.

questions.question: Under which Articles of the Indian Constitution are the Fundamental Rights guaranteed?
questions.explanation: Fundamental Rights are enshrined in Part III, Articles 12 to 35 of the Constitution.

questions.question: The Directive Principles of State Policy were borrowed from the Constitution of which country?
questions.explanation: The concept of Directive Principles of State Policy was borrowed from the Irish Constitution.

questions.question: Which Schedule of the Indian Constitution contains provisions related to Panchayati Raj?
questions.explanation: The Eleventh Schedule (Article 243G) lists the subjects for Panchayati Raj institutions.

questions.question: The Election Commission of India is established under which Article of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 324 provides for the Election Commission of India to supervise elections.

questions.question: The Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj Act, 1994 provides for a three-tier system. What is the correct order from lowest to highest?
options: Gram Panchayat, Panchayat Union, District Panchayat
Panchayat Union, Gram Panchayat, District Panchayat
District Panchayat, Panchayat Union, Gram Panchayat
Gram Panchayat, District Panchayat, Panchayat Union
questions.explanation: The three tiers are Gram Panchayat (village), Panchayat Union (block), and District Panchayat (district).

questions.question: The concept of Judicial Review in India is derived from which country's constitution?
questions.explanation: Judicial Review was adopted from the USA, where it was established by Marbury v. Madison.

questions.question: What is the minimum age for voting in India as per Article 326?
questions.explanation: The 61st Amendment (1988) reduced the voting age from 21 to 18 years.

questions.question: What is the current total number of elected members in the Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly?
questions.explanation: The Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly has 234 elected members, as per the Delimitation Commission.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Constitution are the Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSPs) enumerated?
questions.explanation: DPSPs are contained in Part IV of the Constitution, covering Articles 36 to 51.

questions.question: In the context of Panchayati Raj in Tamil Nadu, what is the minimum age prescribed for contesting elections to a Panchayat?
questions.explanation: As per the Tamil Nadu Panchayats Act, 1994, the minimum age to contest Panchayat elections is 21 years.

questions.question: Which Tamil Nadu government scheme provides free breakfast to students in government primary schools?
questions.explanation: The Chief Minister's Breakfast Scheme was launched in 2022 to provide free nutritious breakfast to primary school students in government schools.

questions.question: Under the Constitution, the Governor of a State is appointed by whom?
questions.explanation: The Governor is appointed by the President of India and holds office during the pleasure of the President.

questions.question: Which of the following is a 'writ' issued by a court to prevent a public authority from acting beyond its powers?
questions.explanation: Prohibition is a writ issued by a higher court to prevent a lower court or tribunal from exceeding its jurisdiction.

questions.question: Which term is NOT explicitly mentioned in the Preamble to the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Preamble declares India as a Sovereign, Socialist, Secular, Democratic Republic but does not mention 'Federal'.

questions.question: Which of the following Fundamental Rights is available only to Indian citizens?
questions.explanation: Article 19 guarantees freedom of speech, assembly, etc., only to citizens, while Articles 14, 21, and 32 apply to all persons.

questions.question: The concept of Directive Principles of State Policy in the Indian Constitution is borrowed from which country?
questions.explanation: The DPSP were inspired by the Irish Constitution, which itself drew from the Spanish Constitution.

questions.question: Which of the following is a feature of the 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act regarding Panchayati Raj?
options: Reservation for women at least one-third
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment mandates reservation for women (not less than one-third) and a fixed five-year term; District Planning Committee is under the 74th Amendment.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the Election Commission of India is correct?
options: It has the power to delimit constituencies
The Chief Election Commissioner can be removed by the President on Parliament's recommendation
It conducts elections only for Lok Sabha and State Assemblies
questions.explanation: The CEC can be removed like a Supreme Court judge, i.e., by the President on an address by Parliament. Delimitation is done by a separate commission.

questions.question: Which of the following is a unique feature of the Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj system?
options: Direct election of Gram Panchayat President
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu provides for direct election of the Gram Panchayat President, unlike many states where the President is elected indirectly.

questions.question: The concept of 'Judicial Review' in India is derived from which country?
questions.explanation: Judicial review, the power to declare laws unconstitutional, is borrowed from the US Constitution.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution directs the state to secure a Uniform Civil Code?
questions.explanation: Article 44, a Directive Principle, urges the state to endeavour to secure a Uniform Civil Code for all citizens.

questions.question: The Tamil Nadu government's 'Chief Minister's Breakfast Scheme' is primarily an example of which type of policy?
questions.explanation: The scheme provides nutritious breakfast to primary school children, aiming to improve nutrition and school attendance, thus a social welfare measure.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a constitutional body?
questions.explanation: NITI Aayog is a statutory body (created by an executive resolution), while Finance Commission, Election Commission, and UPSC are constitutional bodies.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the composition of Panchayats?
questions.explanation: Article 243C specifies the composition of Panchayats.

questions.question: The Preamble to the Constitution of India was adopted on which date?
questions.explanation: The Preamble was adopted on 26 November 1949 along with the Constitution.

questions.question: Which Fundamental Right is available only to citizens and not to foreigners?
questions.explanation: Article 15 prohibits discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, etc. and is available only to citizens.

questions.question: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment added which part to the Constitution?
questions.explanation: 73rd Amendment added Part IX dealing with Panchayats.

questions.question: Which of the following is a flagship healthcare scheme launched by the Tamil Nadu government?
options: Chief Minister's Comprehensive Health Insurance Scheme
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu's Chief Minister's Comprehensive Health Insurance Scheme provides coverage for major illnesses.

questions.question: The power of the Supreme Court to review its own judgement is called?
questions.explanation: Article 137 gives the Supreme Court power to review its own judgments.

questions.question: Which part of the Indian Constitution deals with Fundamental Duties?
questions.explanation: Fundamental Duties were added by 42nd Amendment as Part IVA.

questions.question: The concept of 'Welfare State' is reflected in which part of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Directive Principles aim to establish a welfare state by providing social and economic justice.

questions.question: Which of the following is a feature of the Preamble of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Preamble declares India as a Sovereign, Socialist, Secular, Democratic Republic.

questions.question: The Directive Principles of State Policy are borrowed from which country's constitution?
questions.explanation: The concept of DPSP was borrowed from the Irish Constitution.

questions.question: Which constitutional amendment introduced the Panchayati Raj system in India?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment Act, 1992 gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj institutions.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a function of the Supreme Court of India?
questions.explanation: Impeachment of President is done by Parliament, not Supreme Court.

questions.question: The Tamil Nadu government's 'Amma Unavagam' scheme is an example of which type of governance?
questions.explanation: Amma Unavagam is a subsidized food scheme, reflecting welfare governance.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution deals with the appointment of the Governor of a state?
questions.explanation: Article 155 states that the Governor is appointed by the President.

questions.question: Which of the following is a Tamil Nadu state policy related to local self-government?
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu enacted its own Panchayati Raj Act in 1994 to implement the 73rd Amendment.

questions.question: Which part of the Indian Constitution contains the Directive Principles of State Policy?
questions.explanation: Directive Principles of State Policy are enshrined in Part IV (Articles 36-51) of the Indian Constitution.

questions.question: The concept of 'Welfare State' in the Indian Constitution is reflected in which of the following?
questions.explanation: Directive Principles aim to establish a welfare state by directing the state to secure social and economic justice.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution provides for the establishment of Panchayats?
questions.explanation: Article 243 defines Panchayats and forms the basis of Part IX added by the 73rd Amendment.

questions.question: Which of the following is a feature of the Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj system?
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu follows a two-tier Panchayati Raj system (District Panchayat and Village Panchayat).

questions.question: The power to amend the Constitution of India is vested in which body?
questions.explanation: Under Article 368, Parliament has the power to amend the Constitution subject to prescribed procedures.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a Fundamental Right under the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Right to Property was removed from Fundamental Rights by the 44th Amendment and is now a legal right under Article 300A.

questions.question: Which of the following is a Tamil Nadu state government policy aimed at social justice?
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu pioneered the Mid-day Meal Scheme in 1982 to improve nutrition and school attendance, a key social justice policy.

questions.question: Which of the following words was added to the Preamble by the 42nd Amendment?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) added 'Socialist', 'Secular', and 'Integrity' to the Preamble.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the Right to Constitutional Remedies?
questions.explanation: Article 32 is the 'heart and soul' of the Constitution, providing the right to move the Supreme Court for enforcement of Fundamental Rights.

questions.question: Which of the following Directive Principles of State Policy is based on Gandhian principles?
questions.explanation: Article 48 (Organization of agriculture and animal husbandry) includes prohibition of cow slaughter, reflecting Gandhian principles.

questions.question: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act added which part to the Constitution of India?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment (1992) added Part IX (The Panchayats) to the Constitution.

questions.question: Which Tamil Nadu government scheme provides free breakfast to school children?
questions.explanation: The Chief Minister's Breakfast Scheme was launched in 2022-23 to provide free breakfast to students in government schools in Tamil Nadu.

questions.question: Which of the following features of the Indian Constitution is considered the 'soul' and is not subject to the amending power of Parliament under Article 368?
questions.explanation: The basic structure doctrine, established in Kesavananda Bharati case, holds that certain core features like the supremacy of the Constitution cannot be amended.

questions.question: In the context of Panchayati Raj, which Article of the Indian Constitution mandates the establishment of a State Finance Commission to review the financial position of Panchayats?
questions.explanation: Article 243-I requires the Governor to appoint a State Finance Commission every five years to recommend distribution of funds between the state and Panchayats.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about the Preamble of the Indian Constitution: (1) It was adopted on 26 November 1949, (2) It declares India as a 'Sovereign Socialist Secular Democratic Republic'. Which is correct?
questions.explanation: The Preamble was adopted on 26 November 1949, and the words 'Socialist' and 'Secular' were added by the 42nd Amendment in 1976.

questions.question: Under the Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj Act, 1994, what is the minimum age required for a person to be eligible for election as a member of a Gram Panchayat?
questions.explanation: The Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj Act sets the minimum age at 21 years for contesting elections to Gram Panchayat, Ward, or District Panchayat.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a feature of the Directive Principles of State Policy as enshrined in Part IV of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Directive Principles are non-justiciable, meaning they cannot be enforced by courts, unlike Fundamental Rights which are justiciable.

questions.question: In the Indian judiciary, which writ is issued to a lower court or tribunal to transfer a case pending before it to a higher court?
questions.explanation: Certiorari is issued by a higher court to quash the order of a lower court or to transfer a case to itself for review.

questions.question: Which amendment to the Indian Constitution introduced the concept of 'Anti-Defection Law' and added the Tenth Schedule?
questions.explanation: The 52nd Amendment Act, 1985, added the Tenth Schedule to the Constitution, dealing with disqualification of members on grounds of defection.

questions.question: Under the Election Commission of India, what is the maximum number of Chief Election Commissioners (CEC) that can be appointed at a time as per the current law?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission consists of one Chief Election Commissioner and two Election Commissioners, as per the Election Commission (Conditions of Service) Act, 1991.

questions.question: Which Tamil Nadu state policy is directly linked to the implementation of the 'Right to Education Act, 2009' for ensuring universal access to schooling?
questions.explanation: Samacheer Kalvi (Uniform System of Education) was introduced in Tamil Nadu to align with the RTE Act, ensuring equal curriculum for all students up to Class 12.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a ground for disqualification of a Member of Parliament under the Representation of the People Act, 1951?
options: Being a member of a political party
questions.explanation: Being a member of a political party is not a ground for disqualification; disqualification arises from defection under the Tenth Schedule, not mere party membership.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the Preamble of the Indian Constitution is correct?
options: It was adopted on 26 November 1949 and declares India a socialist, secular, democratic republic.
It was amended in 1976 to add the words 'socialist' and 'secular'.
It is enforceable in a court of law as a source of fundamental rights.
It declares India a federation with unitary features.
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) inserted 'socialist' and 'secular' into the Preamble; it is not enforceable in court.

questions.question: Under Article 243G of the Constitution, which of the following powers is vested in Panchayats?
options: Power to levy taxes exclusively on professional tax.
Power to prepare plans for economic development and social justice.
Power to amend state laws on land reforms.
Power to dissolve state legislative assemblies.
questions.explanation: Article 243G empowers Panchayats to prepare plans for economic development and social justice as per the 11th Schedule.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements about the Election Commission of India (ECI):
1. It conducts elections to Parliament, state legislatures, and local bodies.
2. The Chief Election Commissioner can be removed only through impeachment.
Which is/are correct?
questions.explanation: ECI conducts elections to Parliament and state legislatures, not local bodies; CEC removal is by impeachment-like process.

questions.question: Which of the following is a Directive Principle of State Policy under Part IV of the Constitution?
options: Equal pay for equal work for both men and women.
questions.explanation: Article 39(d) mandates equal pay for equal work for men and women, a Directive Principle, not a Fundamental Right.

questions.question: In the context of Tamil Nadu's local self-government, which of the following is correct about the District Planning Committee (DPC)?
options: It is chaired by the District Collector.
It consolidates plans prepared by Panchayats and Municipalities.
It is a constitutional body under Article 243ZD.
It has no role in resource allocation.
questions.explanation: Article 243ZD mandates DPCs to consolidate plans from rural and urban local bodies; chairperson is elected from among members.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the 'basic structure' doctrine as upheld by the Supreme Court?
options: Parliament can amend any part of the Constitution including Fundamental Rights.
The Preamble is not part of the Constitution.
Certain core features of the Constitution cannot be destroyed by amendment.
Judicial review is limited to procedural matters.
questions.explanation: The basic structure doctrine, from Kesavananda Bharati (1973), holds that core features like secularism cannot be abrogated.

questions.question: Under the Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj Act, 1994, what is the minimum age for a person to contest elections to a Panchayat?
questions.explanation: The Act specifies 21 years as the minimum age for contesting Panchayat elections, aligning with Article 243F.

questions.question: Which of the following is a unique feature of the Indian judiciary compared to the US model?
options: Supreme Court has original jurisdiction in disputes between states.
High Courts have power of judicial review over central laws.
Integrated judiciary with Supreme Court at apex.
Judges are appointed by the President alone.
questions.explanation: India has a single integrated judiciary (Supreme Court to lower courts), unlike the US dual system.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the 73rd Constitutional Amendment is correct?
options: It made Panchayats a permanent part of the Constitution.
It reserved seats for women only in urban local bodies.
It abolished the State Election Commission.
It gave Panchayats power to levy income tax.
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment (1992) added Part IX, making Panchayats constitutional bodies with fixed tenure and reservations.

questions.question: In the context of Tamil Nadu's governance, which of the following is a key feature of the 'Tamil Nadu Model' of development?
options: Heavy reliance on central government grants.
Focus on universal social welfare schemes like mid-day meals.
Privatization of all public sector undertakings.
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu's model emphasizes universal social welfare, notably the pioneering mid-day meal scheme, ensuring high human development.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the Preamble to the Constitution of India is correct?
options: It was amended in 1976 to add 'Socialist' and 'Secular'
It is not a part of the Constitution
It is enforceable in a court of law
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) added 'Socialist' and 'Secular' to the Preamble. The Preamble is part of the Constitution but not enforceable.

questions.question: Under which of the following Articles can the State impose reasonable restrictions on freedom of speech and expression on grounds of public order?
questions.explanation: Article 19(2) specifies the grounds for reasonable restrictions on Article 19(1)(a), including public order.

questions.question: Which of the following Directive Principles is based on Gandhian ideology?
questions.explanation: Article 48 (prohibition of cow slaughter) is a Gandhian principle. Others are socialist or liberal in nature.

questions.question: As per the 73rd Constitutional Amendment, the minimum age for being a member of a Panchayat is:
questions.explanation: Article 243F sets the minimum age at 21 years, the same as for State Legislature membership.

questions.question: Which Constitutional Amendment Act reduced the voting age from 21 to 18 years?
questions.explanation: The 61st Amendment (1988) lowered the voting age for Lok Sabha and State Assembly elections to 18.

questions.question: The concept of 'basic structure' of the Constitution was propounded in which case?
options: Kesavananda Bharati v. State of Kerala
Minerva Mills v. Union of India
S.R. Bommai v. Union of India
questions.explanation: Kesavananda Bharati (1973) established the basic structure doctrine, limiting Parliament's amending power.

questions.question: Which of the following Tamil Nadu government schemes is aimed at providing nutritious breakfast to primary school children?
questions.explanation: The Chief Minister's Breakfast Scheme (2022) provides breakfast to primary school students. The Nutritious Meal Programme covers lunch.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a mandatory provision under the 73rd Constitutional Amendment?
questions.explanation: Reservation for OBCs is optional for states, while SC/ST reservation, five-year tenure, and State Election Commission are mandatory.

questions.question: The Governor of a State can reserve a Bill for the consideration of the President under which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 200 empowers the Governor to reserve a Bill for the President. Article 201 deals with the President's action on such a Bill.

questions.question: Which Fundamental Duty was added by the 86th Constitutional Amendment Act, 2002?
options: To provide opportunities for education to children
questions.explanation: The 86th Amendment inserted Article 51A(k) – duty of parents/guardians to provide educational opportunities to children aged 6–14.

questions.question: Under the 73rd Constitutional Amendment, which of the following is mandatory for a Panchayat to continue in office?
options: Dissolution before term ends by state law.
Elections within six months of dissolution.
Approval of Gram Sabha for extension.
questions.explanation: Article 243E mandates elections within six months of dissolution to ensure continuity of the Panchayat.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the nature of Directive Principles of State Policy?
options: They are justiciable and enforceable by courts.
They are fundamental to governance but non-justiciable.
They override Fundamental Rights in case of conflict.
They can be amended only by a special majority.
questions.explanation: Directive Principles are non-justiciable guidelines for the state, as per Article 37, but are fundamental in governance.

questions.question: In the context of Tamil Nadu, which of the following is a unique feature of the state's Panchayati Raj system?
options: Direct election of District Panchayat Chairpersons.
Gram Sabha meetings held every six months.
State Election Commission appointed by Governor.
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu uniquely provides for direct election of District Panchayat Chairpersons, unlike many other states.

questions.question: Which of the following statements regarding the Election Commission of India is correct?
options: Its members are appointed by the President.
It conducts elections only for Lok Sabha and state assemblies.
Its decisions are subject to judicial review.
questions.explanation: The President appoints the Chief Election Commissioner and other Election Commissioners under Article 324.

questions.question: The concept of 'Basic Structure' of the Constitution was first propounded in which case?
options: A.K. Gopalan v. State of Madras
Kesavananda Bharati v. State of Kerala
Minerva Mills v. Union of India
S.R. Bommai v. Union of India
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court in Kesavananda Bharati (1973) held that the basic structure of the Constitution cannot be amended.

questions.question: Which of the following is a correct statement about the Tamil Nadu State Election Commission?
options: It is a constitutional body under Article 243K.
It is appointed by the Chief Minister.
It conducts elections only to local bodies.
Its orders are not subject to judicial review.
questions.explanation: The State Election Commission conducts elections to Panchayats and municipalities, as per Article 243K.

questions.question: Which of the following Fundamental Rights is available only to citizens of India?
options: Right to freedom of speech and expression.
Right to protection against double jeopardy.
questions.explanation: Article 19 (freedom of speech, etc.) is specifically guaranteed to citizens, not to all persons.

questions.question: In the Indian judicial system, the power of judicial review is derived from which of the following?
questions.explanation: Judicial review flows from Articles 13, 32, and 226, enabling courts to strike down laws violating fundamental rights.

questions.question: Which of the following Tamil Nadu government policies is directly linked to the Directive Principles of State Policy?
options: Mid-day meal scheme for school children.
questions.explanation: The mid-day meal scheme aligns with Article 39(e) and 47, promoting nutrition and health of children.

questions.question: Under Article 21A, free and compulsory education is guaranteed for children in which age group?
questions.explanation: Article 21A guarantees free and compulsory education for children aged 6 to 14 years.

questions.question: Which Directive Principle of State Policy is based on Gandhian principles?
questions.explanation: Article 40, which directs the state to organise village panchayats, is a Gandhian directive principle.

questions.question: Which was the first state in India to implement the Panchayati Raj system?
questions.explanation: Rajasthan (Nagaur district) was the first state to implement Panchayati Raj in 1959, followed by Andhra Pradesh.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution provides for the establishment of the Election Commission?
questions.explanation: Article 324 vests superintendence, direction, and control of elections in the Election Commission.

questions.question: What is the retirement age of a judge of the Supreme Court of India?
questions.explanation: Supreme Court judges retire at age 65, while High Court judges retire at 62.

questions.question: Which Tamil Nadu government scheme provides free breakfast to students in government schools?
questions.explanation: The Chief Minister's Breakfast Scheme was launched in 2022 to provide free breakfast to government school students.

questions.question: Which feature of the Indian Constitution is borrowed from the United Kingdom?
questions.explanation: The parliamentary system of government is borrowed from the United Kingdom.

questions.question: Which Fundamental Right is not available to foreign nationals in India?
options: Right to freedom of speech and expression (Article 19)
Right to life and personal liberty (Article 21)
questions.explanation: Article 19 rights (freedom of speech, assembly, etc.) are available only to citizens, not foreigners.

questions.question: Which constitutional amendment added the words 'Socialist', 'Secular' and 'Integrity' to the Preamble of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment Act of 1976 inserted 'Socialist', 'Secular' and 'Integrity' into the Preamble.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the Election Commission of India: 1. The Constitution provides for a Chief Election Commissioner and such other Election Commissioners as the President may decide. 2. The Chief Election Commissioner is appointed by the President. 3. The conditions of service of the Chief Election Commissioner cannot be varied to his disadvantage after appointment. Which of the statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: All three statements are correct as per Article 324 of the Constitution.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution directs the State to organise village panchayats as units of self-government?
questions.explanation: Article 40, a Directive Principle, mandates the State to organise village panchayats.

questions.question: The Tamil Nadu Panchayats Act, 1994 introduced a disqualification for candidates in local body elections based on family size. Which of the following correctly describes that provision?
questions.explanation: The Act disqualified persons having more than two children from contesting local body elections, later struck down by the Madras High Court.

questions.question: Which constitutional amendment inserted Article 21A making the right to education a fundamental right for children aged 6 to 14?
questions.explanation: The 86th Amendment Act, 2002 added Article 21A to the Constitution.

questions.question: The concept of 'One Nation One Election' in India was examined by a high-level committee headed by which former President?
questions.explanation: The committee on simultaneous elections was formed in 2022 under former President Ram Nath Kovind.

questions.question: Which feature of the Indian Constitution is borrowed from the Weimar Constitution of Germany?
options: Suspension of Fundamental Rights during Emergency
questions.explanation: The provision for suspension of fundamental rights during an emergency is derived from the Weimar Constitution.

questions.question: Which constitutional amendment gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj institutions?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment Act, 1992 added Part IX to the Constitution, granting constitutional status to Panchayats.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution deals with the appointment of judges of the Supreme Court?
questions.explanation: Article 124 provides for the establishment and constitution of the Supreme Court, including appointment of judges.

questions.question: The Second Administrative Reforms Commission (2005) focused on which key theme in governance?
questions.explanation: The Commission's terms of reference centred on 'Good Governance' and citizen-centric administration.

questions.question: Which Fundamental Right is available to both citizens and non-citizens?
options: Right to freedom of speech and expression
questions.explanation: Article 14 (Right to equality) applies to all persons, while rights like speech and voting are limited to citizens.

questions.question: Which Directive Principle is based on the principle of 'equal pay for equal work'?
questions.explanation: Article 39(d) directs the State to ensure equal pay for equal work for both men and women.

questions.question: Under the 73rd Amendment, what is the minimum age for a person to be a member of a Panchayat?
questions.explanation: Article 243F sets the minimum age for Panchayat membership at 21 years.

questions.question: The power of judicial review in India is explicitly derived from which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 13 empowers courts to declare laws void if they violate Fundamental Rights, forming the basis of judicial review.

questions.question: In which year was the 'Amma Unavagam' (canteen) scheme launched in Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: The Amma Unavagam scheme was launched by the Tamil Nadu government in February 2013.

questions.question: The 73rd Amendment to the Constitution added which Part?
questions.explanation: Part IX (Articles 243 to 243O) deals with Panchayats, added by the 73rd Amendment (1992).

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a function of the District Planning Committee?
options: Consolidation of plans prepared by Panchayats and Municipalities
Preparation of draft development plan for the district
Coordination between rural and urban planning
questions.explanation: The District Planning Committee (Article 243ZD) does not approve the state budget; it focuses on district-level planning.

questions.question: Under Article 15 of the Indian Constitution, which of the following is an exception to the prohibition of discrimination?
questions.explanation: Article 15(3) allows special provisions for women, and Article 15(4) allows reservations for socially and educationally backward classes.

questions.question: Which Directive Principle of State Policy is based on the principle of 'equal pay for equal work'?
questions.explanation: Article 39(d) directs the state to ensure equal pay for equal work for both men and women.

questions.question: Which of the following was mandated by the 73rd Amendment Act concerning Panchayati Raj?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment established State Election Commissions, State Finance Commissions, and mandatory reservation for SC/ST and women.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution vests the superintendence, direction, and control of elections in the Election Commission?
questions.explanation: Article 324 gives the Election Commission the power of superintendence, direction, and control of elections.

questions.question: Which Tamil Nadu government scheme provides a monthly financial assistance of Rs. 1,000 to women heads of families?
questions.explanation: The Kalaignar Magalir Urimai Thittam (2023) provides Rs. 1,000 per month to eligible women heads of families.

questions.question: Under the 74th Amendment, which type of urban local body is mandatory for a transitional area (from rural to urban)?
questions.explanation: Article 243Q defines a Nagar Panchayat for areas in transition from rural to urban.

questions.question: Which of the following Fundamental Rights is available only to citizens and not to foreigners?
questions.explanation: Article 19 (freedom of speech, etc.) is available only to citizens, while Articles 14, 21, and 32 are available to all persons.

questions.question: In the context of the conflict between Fundamental Rights and Directive Principles, the Supreme Court in the Minerva Mills case (1980) held that:
options: Fundamental Rights are subordinate to Directive Principles
Directive Principles are subordinate to Fundamental Rights
Both are complementary and neither can be given primacy
The harmony between the two is a basic feature of the Constitution
questions.explanation: Minerva Mills case established that the harmony between Fundamental Rights and DPSP is a basic feature, striking down the 42nd Amendment's attempt to give primacy to DPSP.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the 73rd Constitutional Amendment:
1. Reservation for women in Panchayats is mandatory.
2. Reservation for OBCs is mandatory.
3. The State Election Commission is a mandatory body.
Which of the statements is/are correct?
